title: Error Handling
description: ErrorBoundary, catchError, and handler try/catch keep a failing resource from taking the whole tree down.
error_boundary_intro: <0>ErrorBoundary</0> catches errors thrown in its children and renders a fallback. The uptime monitor wraps its probes in one and renders <1>MonitorFault</1>, which logs that checks halted instead of crashing the process.
catch_error_intro: For finer control, <0>catchError</0> wraps a single computation. The status page renders its HTML through <0>catchError</0>, and a failed render returns <1>undefined</1> so the effect skips the publish instead of throwing.
handler_errors_intro: <0>useAsyncOutput</0> handlers run in the deployment loop, so their throws are <1>not</1> caught by <2>ErrorBoundary</2>. The page writer's handler wraps generation in try/catch and records the failure as durable output instead of letting the deployment throw.
warning_cleanup: Cleanup functions registered with <0>onCleanup</0> still run when an error boundary catches an error, so resources are released.
